followUps.title: Migrate downstream consumers of acronyms-catalog from file/name refs to D9 stream URL
followUps.scope: 5 downstream consumers identified in T1 substrate Step 7: schema-registry/schemas/patent/glossary.schema.json — update $ref to https://schemas.luh.tech/acronyms-catalog.json; LuhTech-business/filings/glossary.md + glossary.yml — link to stream URL; LuhTech-business/docs/LUHTECH-PATENT-FOUNDATION-CORRECTIONS-2026-05-06.md — update reference; LuhTech-business/.roadmap/follow-up-register.json — close stale catalog-absence FU entries.
followUps.coordination: Ectropy-Business follow-up-register should also note this as a cross-repo concern once D9 stream URL is validated live.

followUps.title: Resolve cosmetic jsonschema deprecation warning for decision-log DEC validation
followUps.scope: Step 17 of T2 Phase 1: DEC log validation passes but triggers jsonschema network auto-fetch deprecation warning. Root cause: decision-log.schema.v2 $refs luhtech-enums; the Draft7Validator may not propagate the local registry through to internal sub-validator $ref resolution. Audit referencing.Registry propagation in validate.py; confirm luhtech-enums $id coverage in schema-map; consider explicit pre-resolution walk.
followUps.coordination: Likely folds into T3 substrate prep — fixing propagation lets T3 instance validations run clean. Cosmetic only; validation passes.

resolution.summary: Resolved without code change. Root cause was substrate-ordering during T2 Phase 1: schema-map.json had not yet been generated when DEC log validation ran in Step 17, so the local registry was empty and luhtech-enums $refs fell through to jsonschema network auto-fetch. T2 landed the schema-map; T3 substrate Step 7 reproduced validation under post-T2 registry and observed zero warnings. Lesson: regenerate indices BEFORE running dependent validations.

followUps.title: Bundle XaaS→SNx migration into T5 — directory renames, URN segments, description strings
followUps.scope: T4 substrate confirmed XaaS terminology survives in three Ectropy-domain surfaces: (a) directory names caas/, gaas/, paas/, maas/ holding 6 schemas; (b) URN $id segments encoded into those path components; (c) description-string prose in usf/usf-work-packet, maas/material-supplier, schema-index.json. Bundle into T5 (urn-to-url) so directory rename + URN segment update + description polish land in one coherent mutation pass. Pre-T5: resolve SNx naming-form decision (csx/ vs corsx/ vs full noun); scan Ectropy product code for hard-coded caas/gaas/paas/maas imports that break on rename.
followUps.coordination: SNx naming-form decision needed from Erik before T5 substrate. Also coordinate with Ectropy-Business and BPC on import paths.

resolution.summary: Completed across T5.1 + T5.3 + T5.4. T5.1 (Ectropy-ai/schemas#5) renamed 4 XaaS directories to SNx (caas→corsx, gaas→govsx, paas→paysx, maas→matsx), converted 57 URN $ids to URL form, updated schema-index.json slugs, corrected description prose. T5.3 (Ectropy-Business#143) migrated consumer-side URN→URL. T5.2 struck. T5.4 aligned schema-registry schema-map. Final: 99/99 D2 compliance.

followUps.title: Define deprecation policy + execute migrate v1-to-v3-deprecate for v-suffix families
followUps.scope: T4 substrate Step 4 surfaced 5 multi-variant schema families: luhtech-enums (2 variants), decision-log (2), roadmap (10), roadmap-business (4 incl v3.0.draft), venture-summary (2). Step 5 showed v-suffix variants are not always old: luhtech-enums v2 IS the active one (6 live $refs from graph/brand/workflow-registry). Three policy decisions before execute: (1) deprecation marker form — boolean field, status enum, or _archived/ subdir; (2) per-family current-variant confirmation via $ref evidence; (3) $ref migration plan for consumers of variants to be deprecated.

followUps.title: Remove unauthorized DaaS parenthetical from ectropy-ai/schemas/schema-index.json
followUps.scope: schema-index.json carries 'Decision Lifecycle (DaaS)' as a category name. Erik confirmed: DaaS was never approved — it is not in the canonical catalog section J. The Decision Lifecycle category itself is real (decision/ subdir, 5 schemas); only the (DaaS) parenthetical is wrong. Recommended fix: drop the parenthetical. Bundle into FU-ECTROPY-XAAS-MIGRATION-1 Ectropy editing pass at T5. Also audit VaaS, CaaS, PaaS, MaaS, RaaS/HaaS category name parentheticals for the same issue.
followUps.coordination: Bundled into FU-ECTROPY-XAAS-MIGRATION-1 — do the schema-index.json cleanup in the same T5 editing pass as XaaS directory renames. No new DSx acronym needed — Decision Lifecycle as a category does not need an SNx-style parenthetical.

resolution.summary: Resolved during T5.1 schema-index slug migration. DaaS slug renamed to 'decision' (directory-aligned; decision/ schemas are data primitives, not service exchanges). VaaS slug renamed to 'voxel' by the same logic. DaaS parenthetical stripped from the 'Decision Lifecycle' category display name. No DSx acronym added to catalog because 'Decision Lifecycle' names a schema primitive tier, not a service-exchange interface.

followUps.title: Review ventureType enum — propose 5-bucket consolidation (Platform/Hardware/Service/Marketplace/Holding)
followUps.scope: Per D-T5-C (deferred from T5 arc): luhtech-enums.schema.v2.json ventureType enum includes SaaS, API, Platform, Hardware, Marketplace, Holding. Proposed 5-bucket consolidation: Platform | Hardware | Service | Marketplace | Holding — absorbs SaaS + API into Service (broader model covering SaaS, managed-services, professional-services). Requires: audit current usage across 8 venture roadmap.json files; draft migration mapping (SaaS→Service, API→Platform); file DEC with breaking-change semantics; schema change additive then deprecation pass.
